reasoning: As of early December 2025, Elon Musk remains the CEO of Tesla. In November 2025, Tesla shareholders approved an enormous new pay package for Musk (with targets reaching up to $1 trillion), strongly incentivizing him to remain at the helm and hit long-term goals. While there was some shareholder and employee pressure earlier in the year (e.g., around March and May 2025), he instead stepped down from his government "DOGE" role in May 2025, which reduced his external commitments and quieted concerns about his divided attention. The Manifold market was at 0.02 on November 27, 2025. With less than a month remaining in the year, the likelihood of a sudden departure, resignation, or death before the end of 2025 is exceptionally low. I align with the market consensus of 2%.
